sourceMovie.title: Out of Sight
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Out of Sight (1998) stuck because it commits to its romance premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Steven Soderbergh's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The romance and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Old Man & the Gun
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Out of Sight clicked for you, The Old Man & the Gun (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Out of Sight?
faq.answer: Try The Old Man & the Gun, Bottle Rocket, Bandits—each shares DNA with Out of Sight while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Out of Sight?
faq.answer: Out of Sight (1998) is best described as Romance, Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Out of Sight worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
